testimonies.speaker: PLACEHOLDER — Replace with real narrator name
testimonies.transcript: PLACEHOLDER TRANSCRIPT. We used to come to this tank every morning. It was the centre of our neighbourhood. I remember the smell of marigold offerings. After Partition, we never came back. I am told it is still there, but filled with debris now. Replace this with a real testimony from The 1947 Partition Archive.

testimonies.speaker: PLACEHOLDER — Replace with real narrator name
testimonies.transcript: PLACEHOLDER TRANSCRIPT. My grandfather built that house in 1912. By August 15th it belonged to someone else. Not because it was taken — because we ran. We thought we would return in a few weeks. We never did. Replace this with a real testimony from The 1947 Partition Archive.

testimonies.transcript: PLACEHOLDER TRANSCRIPT. The dharamsala had been a resting place for pilgrims for 200 years. By 1948 they had built a mosque on the same ground. I do not say this with anger — we did the same things on the other side of the border. But the erasure is real. Replace this with a real testimony from The 1947 Partition Archive.
